summary: Resolve Action Workflow for Director Assigned Actions - All tests passed. Backend 8/8 pytest tests passed. Frontend UI verified: Resolve modal opens correctly with all required fields (resolution notes, notify director checkbox, add to timeline checkbox, follow-up task input), 'From undefined' bug is FIXED (now shows 'Escalated by Coach Williams'), Cancel closes modal, Submit resolves action and shows toast.
seed_data_creation: Created test escalations via POST /api/support-pods/athlete_1/escalate during testing
context_for_next_testing_agent: Resolve Action workflow fully tested. Key findings: 1) 'From undefined' bug is FIXED - coach escalations now show 'Escalated by [coach_name]', director-created actions show 'From [director_name]'. 2) Resolve modal opens with data-testid='resolve-action-modal'. 3) Modal shows: action context (reason_label + note), resolution summary textarea (required), Notify director checkbox (checked by default), Add to athlete timeline checkbox (checked by default), Create follow-up task input (optional). 4) Submit disabled when notes empty, enabled after filling. 5) Cancel button closes modal without action. 6) Submit moves action to Resolved, shows toast 'Resolved with follow-up created' when follow-up provided. 7) Backend POST /api/director/actions/{id}/resolve accepts {note, notify_director, add_to_timeline, follow_up_title} and returns {action_id, status, follow_up_id}. 8) Already-resolved actions return 400.

test_classes: TestResolveActionEndpoint - resolve endpoint with all new fields

from_undefined_fix.details: 'From undefined' NOT present, 'Escalated by Coach Williams' shown for coach escalations

modal_context_shown.details: Shows reason_label ('Escalation: Needs director intervention') and note

submit_disabled_when_empty.details: Submit button disabled when resolution notes empty

cancel_closes_modal.details: Cancel button closes modal without resolving

submit_resolves_action.details: Action moves to Resolved section, toast shows success

toast_notification.details: 'Resolved with follow-up created' shown when follow-up provided
